comic_description: Where it all began. Genius.

Recently split from a 3 year relationship with excessive energy from my simmering storehouse of unprocessed emotional fodder, I drew a series of tragic accounts of old girlfriends. This massive 52 pg issue is chock-full of crudely drawn sex and nudity, including Nate wandering about led by his incessant, mindless erection.
comic_info: So, here I am living on Orcas in the Electric Greenhouse on the remote east end of the island. I am two months into my landscaping season with Victor and my girlfriend Samantha is going to school in Portland. Then I get that call: "..maybe we should see other people.."

comic_description: A potent ingredient in Brainfag Take-Over-The-World Cake. Tuck has it rough. Every time he gets back on his feet, he\\'s suddenly & inexplicably lost in a inky black abyss. The wily octopus awaits around every corner and sexy spiderwomen beg him to put down his walking stick and roll in the hay. Resist, Tuck!
comic_info:
Brainfag 4 "TUCK" was started as a stream-of-consciousness journal in my word-notebook. Beginning with direct inking on thin, lined notebook paper, it grew into pencilling & ink-washing on bristol.

When I find the random inspiration and avoid the general depression clouding my brain lately, I\\'ll move on to coloring these. For now, you\\'ll have to enjoy them in grayscale.

I have mixed feelings about this comic. I do like a few of the pages. However, most pages induce vague emotional response. This is appropriate as they were originally inspired by vague emotions.

Lately, the women in my life have inspired nothing in me but spite. Therefore, I have no reservation in presenting Tuck attacking the lecherous female spider creatures. Sklorp away Tuck!

-Nathan (1/20/02)

comic_description: A chronicle of my tumultuous relationship with Abigail, spawning 44 pages. Written in the midst of a confusing breakup, BF5 explores the difficulty humans experience when they really like eachother but can\\'t seem to make things work. Sub-distributed by Top Shelf Productions.
comic_info:
For some reason, I decided to draw my nose as a big, blocky snout in this issue. I can just see all the girls absorbed with psychology buzzing in their seats.

Abigail was a little hesitant about me publishing this & I\\'ve never published anything so recently relevant to my life, at least anything of such emotional clamor.

Such is the consequence of dating a boy who creatively chews his life\\'s cud continually. Woo, I crack myself up.

-Nathan (1/29/02)

comic_description: The issue that most people in the comic business say \\\I really like *this* one\\\" -- WHATEVER! "

I pencilled, inked & watercolored this children\\'s book for my nephew, Aaron, in 18 hours. I scanned the b&w pages before coloring and made minis (what\\'s offered for sale), then colored and scanned the finished book, which you can view online.
comic_info:
The concept for flyth has been rattling around my brain since 1997, starting as an abstract drawing I did on a work break.

Five years later I pencilled & inked this little book in 12 hours. The next day I spent 6 hours watercoloring it.

This was two days after the incredibly stressful 2002 Portland Zine Symposium which I helped organize. I had also just rushed to finish the two Brainfag 6 minis, as well as the first issue of Shrike. I didn\\'t sleep much that week.

The watercolored & hand-sewn book was a birthday gift to my nephew Aaron, who shares my birthday and had just turned six.

- Nathan (7/19/02)
